en.extract:
The Okinawa robin (Larvivora namiyei) is a passerine bird endemic to Okinawa of Japan. It previously was considered a subspecies of the Ryukyu robin (Larvivora komadori ).


Behavior
Sometimes forages for food near the ground. Predation by invasive species such as the small Indian mongoose negatively impacts the Okinawa Robin.


References

The '''Okinawa robin''' ('''''Larvivora namiyei''''') is a [[passerine]] bird [[endemic]] to [[Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa]] of [[Japan]].<ref>{{cite book|editor=Collar, N. J.|editor2=Andreev, A. V.|editor3=Chan, S.|editor4=Crosby, M. J.|editor5=Subramanya, S.|editor6=Tobias, J. A. |year=2001|contribution= Ryukyu Robin|title=Threatened Birds of Asia: The BirdLife International Red Data Book|publisher=BirdLife International|isbn= 0-946888-44-2|url=http://www.rdb.or.id/detailbird.php?id=704|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070224194324/http://www.rdb.or.id/detailbird.php?id=704|archive-date=24 February 2007}}</ref> It previously was considered a subspecies of the [[Ryukyu robin]] (''Larvivora komadori '').<ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-06-13|language=en-US}}</ref>

== Behavior ==
Sometimes forages for food near the ground. Predation by invasive species such as the [[small Indian mongoose]] negatively impacts the Okinawa Robin.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Yagihashi |first1=Tsutomu |last2=Seki |first2=Shin-Ichi |last3=Nakaya |first3=Tomoki |last4=Nakata |first4=Katsushi |last5=Kotaka |first5=Nobuhiko |date=2021-07-01 |title=Eradication of the mongoose is crucial for the conservation of three endemic bird species in Yambaru, Okinawa Island, Japan |journal=Biological Invasions |language=en |volume=23 |issue=7 |pages=2249–2260 |doi=10.1007/s10530-021-02503-w |issn=1573-1464|doi-access=free }}</ref>
